{"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"institution":[{"name":"ScienceOpen"}],"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,23]],"date-time":"2026-01-23T02:24:59Z","timestamp":1769135099664,"version":"3.49.0"},"posted":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,19]]},"reference-count":0,"publisher":"ScienceOpen","license":[{"start":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,19]],"date-time":"2026-01-19T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1768780800000},"content-version":"unspecified","delay-in-days":0,"URL":"http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/"}],"content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":[],"abstract":"<jats:p>
                  This paper completes the Spectral–Geometric Additive–Multiplicative (AM) regulation series by framing radio pulsar and magnetar observations within explicit information-theoretic limits. Building on earlier demonstrations that AM-regulation stabilizes weak spectral coherence without introducing artificial correlations, the author shows that many long-standing observational ambiguities arise from entropy loss at the survey–analysis interface rather than intrinsic source complexity.
                  <jats:bold>
                    <jats:sup>[1]</jats:sup>
                  </jats:bold>
                  Using pulsar timing archives, magnetar burst datasets, and controlled synthetic surveys; the author quantifies how regulation redistributes information across spectral, temporal, and geometric channels. Demonstrating that AM-regulation operates near optimal recoverability bounds, preserving Shannon information while reducing spurious entropy introduced by thresholding and cadence.
                  <jats:bold>
                    <jats:sup>[1][8]</jats:sup>
                  </jats:bold>
                  The framework provides a conservative bridge between raw radio data and physical inference, clarifying what can and cannot be learned from current and next-generation radio surveys.
                </jats:p>","DOI":"10.14293/pr2199.002819.v1","type":"posted-content","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,22]],"date-time":"2026-01-22T11:35:34Z","timestamp":1769081734000},"source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Spectral–Geometric Regulation and Information-Theoretic Limits in Radio Astronomy"],"prefix":"10.14293","author":[{"ORCID":"https://orcid.org/0009-0000-3496-6047","authenticated-orcid":false,"given":"Savio Antonio","family":"Vogt","sequence":"first","affiliation":[{"id":[{"id":"https://ror.org/04h699437","id-type":"ROR","asserted-by":"publisher"}],"name":"University of Leicester, Leicester, England, United Kingdom (Alma Mater)"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"5403","container-title":[],"original-title":[],"link":[{"URL":"https://scienceopen.com/hosted-document?doi=10.14293/PR2199.002819.v1","content-type":"unspecified","content-version":"vor","intended-application":"similarity-checking"}],"deposited":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,22]],"date-time":"2026-01-22T11:35:34Z","timestamp":1769081734000},"score":1,"resource":{"primary":{"URL":"https://scienceopen.com/hosted-document?doi=10.14293/PR2199.002819.v1"}},"subtitle":["From Survey Bias to Physical Inference"],"short-title":[],"issued":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,19]]},"references-count":0,"URL":"https://doi.org/10.14293/pr2199.002819.v1","relation":{},"subject":[],"published":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,19]]},"subtype":"preprint"}}
